title: Suspect arrested for allegedly shooting 2 city workers in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Local News The 32-year-old suspect, now in custody, is facing two counts of first-degree assault, police say.
A 32-year-old man was arrested Thursday after allegedly shooting two Manchester Department of Public Works employees, authorities announced.
The workers were walking on a sidewalk near Manchester Street and Union Street around 9:20 a.

title: DCR pools are open for the summer across Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News “Every child deserves the opportunity to learn how to swim.
Swimming is more than a summer activity –it's a life-saving skill that helps children safely enjoy our pools, lakes and beaches with confidence,” said DCR Commissioner Nicole LaChapelle.
A lifeguard sat ready on her stand at the Melnea A.

title: Feds announce 15 arrests in alleged $1.4 million public benefits fraud crackdown
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime 11 are accused of living in the US without legal status.
More than a dozen people have been arrested in connection with an alleged scheme that defrauded Massachusetts taxpayers of more than $1.
4 million in public benefits.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox rally then fall, 4-3, to Blue Jays to seal sweep
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Boston Red Sox Boston has now lost 8 of 10 and is a season-low 14 games below .
Red Sox first baseman Willson Contreras (40) reacts after striking out to end the fourth inning.

title: Smith & Wesson reports surge in firearms sales
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The former Springfield-based firearms maker reported a sharp increase in quarterly sales despite a broader decline in background checks nationwide.
Attendees look at a display of Smith and Wesson handguns during the NRA Annual Meeting.
(Photo by Justin Sullivan/Getty Images) Even as firearm background checks continue to decline from record highs seen during the pandemic, Smith & Wesson is reporting a sharp increase in sales.

title: Enjoy the Red Line now, a big chunk of it will shut down after the World Cup
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News The Red Line will be closed between Alewife and Park Street for 10 days in late July.
Red Line service will be suspended between Alewife and Park Street for 10 straight days this summer.
David Ryan/Boston Globe Boston is currently teeming with visitors in town for the World Cup, who are making frequent use of the city’s transit infrastructure.

title: Esmir Bajraktarević sent his country to a World Cup. He’s also a Revolution academy success story.
author: Seth Macomber
bullets: World Cup Bajraktarević, whose professional career began with the Revolution, is now a starter for Bosnia and Herzegovina.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court as lawyers argue whether jury should hear husband’s ‘horrific’ 911 call
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Crime With Clancy’s murder trial now a month away, both sides argued which evidence may go before the jury.
Lindsay Clancy appeared via Zoom from Tewksbury Hospital for a hearing Tuesday, Sept.
30, 2025, in Plymouth Superior Court in Brockton.
